title: [v4.2.0-rhel] Bump Buildah to v1.27.4 CVE-2024-1753
body:
As the title says.  Bumping Buildah to v1.27.4 to
addresses: CVE-2024-1753

https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-26761

(and probably one other card that I think ProdSec forgot)

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.2.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@vrothberg or @mheon do you or anyone else know what's going on with the go modify in the CI?

```
go: downloading google.golang.org/appengine v1.6.7
go mod vendor
warning: ignoring symlink /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/contrib/systemd/user
internal error: failed to find embedded files of google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protojson: //go:build comment without // +build comment
make: *** [Makefile:279: vendor] Error 1
Exit status: 2
```

Using `make vendor-in-container` in my sandbox works fine and it didn't pick up any more changes....
Newer Go version being used for vendor-in-container, maybe? I would worry about the old Go toolchain here
@cevich can we bump the Go version in this branch and is it advisable?
Crud, I completely scrozzled my local branch, I'm going to kill this and try again from scratch.
